page: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8175M processor running at 3.1 GHz. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. Single-core performance is consistently weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tasks. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle-50% scores in stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Notably, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is strong, placing in the top 25% for both read and write operations, though cached small-block reads remain average.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the m5.16xlarge is defined by its high resource density and balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, though the older DDR4 memory generation and Intel Xeon 8175M architecture present a tradeoff compared to newer hardware generations. It is suited for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and multi-core parallelization rather than raw single-threaded speed. Appropriate workloads include database operations, static web serving, Redis deployments, and moderate multi-threaded compilation or text processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8175M processor. It features 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core capabilities, but average multi-core processing and strong large-block memory bandwidth. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving where memory throughput is critical but single-threaded execution speed is not the primary bottleneck.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 20 Gbps network baseline, delivering strong large-block memory bandwidth.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring sixty-four virtual cores, high memory bandwidth, and a twenty gigabit network baseline.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong large-block read and write bandwidth
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Average multi-core performance alongside weak single-core execution speeds
Suited for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
